attacking_style: Launches sustained attacks from GC groups on final climbs, typically hunting down breakaways in the last kilometer rather than going early. Willing to attack mid-climb for tactical objectives (KOM points) even while in contention.
team_tactics: Minimal domestique reliance visible; benefits from unlikely support (sprinter Matthews securing KOM points). Positioned to capitalize on others chasing, often sitting on stronger riders before final move.
terrain_preference: High-mountain summit finishes (Izoard, Galibier) where pure climbing ability dominates. Exposed on flat sprint finishes after climbs where mistiming leads to fading in final meters.
vulnerability: Timing errors on non-summit finishes (attacked 12km out, faded in sprint). Form inconsistency year-to-year (2017 peak vs 2018 decline by 49 KOM points). Early-season crashes disrupt preparation.
key_patterns: Attacks from GC group on final climbs, not traditional breakaways (3 observations)
Prioritizes KOM jersey over GC position, tactical point collection over placing (3 observations)
Sits on rivals doing chase work, conserves energy for final sprint/move (2 observations)
Peak form highly variable: dominant 2017 climber, significantly weaker 2018 repeat attempt (2 observations)
Mistimes efforts on stages without summit finishes, goes too early (2 observations)
form_trajectory: negative: 2017 showed best pure climber status with multiple wins; 2018 defending KOM significantly weaker (49 points behind); 2020 early crash abandonment suggests continued inconsistency
